A Driver must not operate an Interior Advertising System capable of collecting payment information from a passenger through any tablet. [ALP S-107]

COL.PARA: Fine: $250 if plead guilty before a hearing; $350 if found guilty following a hearing. [ALP S-107]

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016; amended City Record 5/17/2018, eff. 6/16/2018; amended City Record 8/19/2019, eff. 9/18/2019; amended City Record 2/2/2023, eff. 3/4/2023; amended City Record 5/10/2023, eff. 6/9/2023; amended City Record 8/22/2024, eff. 9/21/2024; amended City Record 2/4/2025, eff. 3/6/2025) [ALP S-107]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-18 Operations – Lost Property.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-18 Operations – Lost Property.

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Inspection Following Trips to and from Airports.

Immediately after completing a trip to Kennedy, La Guardia or Newark Airports, the Driver must inspect the interior of the Vehicle and the trunk compartment, if used, to ensure that Passengers have collected their property.

RECORD.PARA: (1)Property found by a Driver in a Taxicab must be returned to the Passenger if possible; otherwise, it must be taken without delay to a police precinct as directed by the NYPD. [ALP S-011]

(2)Property found by a Driver in a For-Hire Vehicle or Street Hail Livery must be returned to the Passenger if possible; otherwise it must be taken without delay to a police precinct as directed by the NYPD or to the Vehicle's Base. [ALP S-011]
(c)A Driver cannot demand or request payment for the return of property [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: If the property is not returned to the Passenger, the Driver must promptly inform the Commission of the details regarding the found property and the location where it is held.

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016) [ALP S-011]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-19 Operations – Passenger Solicitation and Engagement.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-19 Operations – Passenger Solicitation and Engagement.

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Limits on Driver Solicitation of Passengers in Taxicabs

RECORD.PARA: (1)A Driver of a Taxicab must not use a person, other than a dispatcher at a public transportation terminal, licensed E-Hail Application, or an Accessible Vehicle dispatcher, to solicit Passengers. Use of any licensed E-Hail Application by the Driver is optional. [ALP S-041]
(2)A Driver of a Taxicab must not pick up additional Passengers during a trip except at the Passenger's request. [ALP S-011]
(3)A Driver of a Taxicab must not suggest to a current Passenger that an additional person be accepted as a Passenger. [ALP S-011]
(4)A Driver of a Taxicab must not solicit or cruise for the purpose of soliciting Passengers: [ALP S-033]
(i)At Kennedy, La Guardia or Newark Airports. [ALP S-011]
(ii)Within 100 feet of any authorized Taxi Stand. [ALP S-011]
(iii)Within the private streets of Lincoln Center. [ALP S-011]
(iv)In any area of the City of New York where Taxicab cruising is prohibited. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (5)A Driver who is logged off from the Technology System must not solicit or accept a Passenger unless ALL of the following are true: [ALP S-083]
(i)The Driver is returning the Taxicab to his or her garage or home; [ALP S-011]

(ii)The Driver has transmitted the relevant information to an electronic database for entry on the electronic trip record or made a written trip record entry "Returning to garage (or home)"; and [ALP S-011]
(iii)The Passenger's destination is directly on the route to the Driver's home or garage. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $75 if plead guilty before a hearing; $100 if found guilty following a hearing.
Limits on Driver Solicitation of Passengers in Street Hail Liveries.

RECORD.PARA: (i)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery must not solicit Passengers in the Hail Exclusionary Zone. [ALP S-011]
(ii)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery must not accept a Pre-Arranged Trip beginning in the Pre- Arranged Exclusionary Zone. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Second Violation in 24 months: $750 and suspension;

RECORD.PARA: (i)Inside the Hail Zone, a Driver of a Street Hail Livery can accept Passengers by Hail or can accept a Pre-Arranged Trip through a Base. [ALP S-011]
(ii)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery must not solicit or cruise for the purpose of soliciting Passengers within 100 feet of any authorized Taxi Stand. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (iii)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery must not use a person, other than a dispatcher at a public transportation terminal, licensed E-Hail Application, an Accessible Vehicle dispatcher, or a Street Hail Livery Base, to solicit Passengers. Use of any licensed E-Hail Application by the Driver is optional. [ALP S-041]
(3)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery must not pick up additional Passengers during a trip except at the Passenger's request. [ALP S-011]
(4)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery must not suggest to a current Passenger that an additional person be accepted as a Passenger. [ALP S-011]

(5)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery who has indicated through the Technology System that he or she is on-duty unavailable for the purposes of accepting a Pre-Arranged trip must not solicit or accept a Passenger for a Hail Trip. [ALP S-026]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $75 if plead guilty before a hearing; $100 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (6)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery who has indicated in the meter or through the Technology System that he or she is off duty must not solicit or accept a Passenger unless ALL of the following are true: [ALP S-026]
(i)The Driver is in the Hail Zone; [ALP S-011]
(ii)The Driver is returning the Street Hail Livery to his or her Base or home; [ALP S-011]
(iii)The Driver has transmitted the relevant information to an electronic database for entry on the electronic trip record or made a written trip record entry; and [ALP S-011]
(iv)The Passenger's destination is directly on the route to the Driver's home or garage. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $75 if plead guilty before a hearing; $100 if found guilty following a hearing.
Limits on Driver Solicitation of Passengers in For-Hire Vehicles.

RECORD.PARA: (1)A Driver of a For-Hire Vehicle must not solicit or pick up Passengers other than by prearrangement through a licensed Base of the class that can dispatch the Driver's For-Hire Vehicle. [ALP S-057]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Second Violation in 24 months: $350 - $500, and up to 30 days' suspension.

If a Driver solicits or picks up Passengers in Manhattan south of East 96th Street and West 110th Street or at the New York City Airports other than by prearrangement through a licensed Base of the class that can dispatch the Driver's For-Hire Vehicle, or by dispatch of an Accessible Vehicle, the Driver will be subject to the below enhanced penalties.
A Driver of a For-Hire Vehicle must not require that any prospective Passenger share a ride with another prospective Passenger.

RECORD.PARA: (1)A Driver of a Taxicab has the right to take a position at any Taxi Stand having a vacancy, and no other Driver may interfere with that right. [ALP S-011]

(i)A Driver of a Street Hail Livery has the right to take a position at any Taxi Stand having a vacancy in the Hail Zone, and no other Driver may interfere with that right. A Driver of a Street Hail Livery does NOT have the right to take a position at a Taxi Stand in the Hail Exclusionary Zone. [ALP S-011]
(2)A For-Hire Driver must not pick up Passengers at any Taxi Stand. [ALP S-011]
(3)A Driver must not occupy a Taxi Stand in order to repair his or her Vehicle, except for minor emergency repairs. [ALP S-011]
(4)A Driver must take the rear position on the line formed at a Taxi Stand (other than a relief stand) and must not overcrowd such stand. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (5)A Driver must only occupy a Taxi Stand when on duty or for no longer than one hour while on relief time or for Personal Use-Off Duty. [ALP S-011]

(6)The Drivers of each of the first two Vehicles on a Taxi Stand, (other than a relief stand), must remain in the driver's seat ready to accept Passengers. Any other Driver on a Taxi Stand must be no more than 15 feet from his or her Vehicle unless he or she is off duty. [ALP S-011]

(7)A Driver may take any vacant position at a relief stand however Drivers must comply with all posted restrictions including the type(s) of Vehicles that may occupy a position at the relief stand and the maximum time a Vehicle may park at the relief stand. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Taxicab Passenger Pick Up at Airports and Terminals.

RECORD.PARA: (1)The rules regarding Taxi Stands also apply to special Taxi Stands and Feed Lines at air, rail, bus and ship terminals. (Note: These rules also apply to Street Hail Liveries for terminals located in the Hail Zone). [ALP S-011]
(2)Where a terminal provides Taxicab or Street Hail Livery holding areas: [ALP S-011]
(i)Drivers must park the Vehicle in a taxi holding area before leaving on relief time. [ALP S-011]
(ii)Upon returning from relief time, the Driver must join the end of the Feed Line. [ALP S-011]
(iii)Drivers are not permitted to bring Passengers into a holding area. [ALP S-011]
(3)Where an airport Taxi Stand offers both Long Haul and Short Haul lines, if there are Taxicabs available in both lines: [ALP S-011]
(i)A Driver who has been given a Short Haul ticket must not accept a Passenger for a Long Haul. [ALP S-011]
(ii)A Driver who has been given a Long Haul ticket must not accept a Passenger for a Short Haul. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: For-Hire Vehicle Dispatch at Airports and Terminals.

A Driver of a For-Hire Vehicle must not accept a dispatch while parked or otherwise located at Kennedy Airport or La Guardia Airport unless the Driver is parked in an area other than the airport's designated passenger pick up locations.

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016; amended City Record 5/17/2018, eff. 6/16/2018; amended City Record 12/11/2018, eff. 1/10/2019; amended City Record 8/19/2019, eff. 9/18/2019; amended City Record 12/9/2020, eff. 1/8/2021; amended City Record 2/2/2023, eff. 3/4/2023) [ALP S-083]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-20 Operations – Refusing Passengers.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-20 Operations – Refusing Passengers.

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Must Not Refuse to Transport Passengers.
Unless the Driver has justifiable grounds, the Driver must not refuse to transport in any of the following circumstances:

A Driver must not refuse by words, gestures or any other means, to take a Passenger, including a person with a disability and any service animal accompanying this individual, to any destination within the City of New York, the counties of Westchester or Nassau, or Newark Airport.
Mandatory penalties as set forth in 35 RCNY § 80-02(e).
Attendant for Passengers with a Disability.
A Driver must not require a person with a disability to be accompanied by an attendant.
Mandatory penalties as set forth in 35 RCNY § 80-02(e).
A Driver must not refuse to transport a Passenger's luggage, wheelchair, crutches, other mobility aid or other property.
Mandatory penalties as set forth in 35 RCNY § 80-02(e).

A Driver must not attempt to learn the destination of a Passenger before the Passenger is seated in the Taxicab or, for a Hail Trip started in the Hail Zone, in the Street Hail Livery, except for Flex Fare Trips. Attempting to learn the destination in advance is a violation of this paragraph, regardless if the Driver ultimately agrees to transport the Passenger.

RECORD.PARA: (i)Asking for a Passenger's destination in advance shall not be a violation of this paragraph if the Driver is returning to his or her home or garage and has followed the procedures provided in 35 RCNY § 80-19(a)(5) or 35 RCNY § 80-19(b)(6). [ALP S-101]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Mandatory penalties as set forth in 35 RCNY § 80-02(e).
A Driver must not refuse to transport a Passenger after accepting a Hail from that Passenger by means of an E-Hail Application.
Mandatory penalties as set forth in 35 RCNY § 80-02(e).
Accessible Dispatch Program and E-Hail Requests.

(i)While operating an Accessible Vehicle, a Driver, after accepting any E-Hail based on the metered rate of fare received through the Technology System or from licensed E-Hail Application providers or from the Accessible Taxi Dispatcher, must not refuse to transport a Passenger.

(ii)A Driver who does not provide service after accepting an E-Hail Request based on the metered rate of fare has refused to provide service and will be subject to Mandatory Penalties for a refusal under subdivision (e) of this section.
(iii)A Driver must not operate an Accessible Vehicle unless the Technology System is turned on.
Mandatory penalties as set forth in 35 RCNY § 80-02(e).

RECORD.PARA: (iv) Assisting the Passenger. A Driver [ALP S-109]
(A)Must assist a passenger who uses a wheelchair or a passenger with other disabilities when: [ALP S-109]
(I)Entering the vehicle from the sidewalk; and [ALP S-109]
(II)Exiting from the vehicle to the curbside. [ALP S-109]
(B)Must secure a passenger with a disability and their mobility device within the Vehicle in a manner as specified in an approved TLC Education Provider training course. [ALP S-109]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $50 and suspension if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing and suspension. The suspension is deferred for 60 days if the Driver completes the Vision Zero and Accessibility Remedial course within the 60-day period, and the Driver will not be suspended.

RECORD.PARA: (v) Packages. Upon a Passenger’s request, a Driver must place the packages and parcels of a passenger with a disability in the Vehicle and secure them and must retrieve them for the Passenger at the end of the trip. [ALP S-109]

COL.PARA: Fine: $50 and suspension if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing and suspension. The suspension is deferred for 60 days if the Driver completes the Vision Zero and Accessibility Remedial course within the 60-day period, and the Driver will not be suspended.

RECORD.PARA: (vi) Service Animal(s) and Companions. A Driver must accept and provide transportation in the Accessible Taxicab for the Service Animal(s) of a passenger with a disability and for as many companions as can be legally seated in the vehicle. [ALP S-109]

COL.PARA:
Fine: Mandatory Penalties as set forth in
35 RCNY § 80-02(e).

RECORD.PARA: (vii) Assist with payment. Upon a Passenger’s request, the Driver must assist the Passenger with completing payment, including but not limited to helping the Passenger to access the credit card reader and counting cash aloud. [ALP S-109]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: The following are permitted reasons for refusing to transport a Passenger:

RECORD.PARA: (1)Another Passenger is already seated in the Vehicle. [ALP S-011]

(2)The Driver has already acknowledged a Hail from another person, and that other person is being picked up or is about to be picked up. (CAUTION: A Driver must not acknowledge the Hail of one person over another in order to avoid transporting the person whose Hail was not acknowledged.) [ALP S-011]

(3)The Passenger is carrying or is in possession of any article, package, case or container which the Driver reasonably believes will cause damage to the Vehicle. (CAUTION: This does NOT include wheelchairs, crutches, a service animal or other mobility aid used by a Passenger with a disability. Mobility aids must be accepted.) [ALP S-011]
(4)The Taxicab or Street Hail Livery Driver is discharging his or her last Passenger or Passengers prior to logging off from the Technology System. [ALP S-083]
(5)The Taxicab or Street Hail Livery Driver is ending his or her work shift, and has already logged off from the Technology System and locked both rear doors. [ALP S-083]
(6)The Taxicab or Street Hail Livery Driver must take the Vehicle out of service for required repairs and has already logged off from the Technology System and locked both rear doors. [ALP S-083]
(7)The Passenger is accompanied by an animal that is not properly secured in a suitable container. (CAUTION: This does not apply to service animals accompanying people with disabilities.) [ALP S-011]

(8)The Passenger's destination is Newark Airport or someplace in Nassau or Westchester County, and the Driver has been operating the Vehicle for more than eight hours of any continuous 24-hour period. [ALP S-011]
(9)The Passenger is disorderly or intoxicated. (CAUTION: Drivers must not refuse to provide service solely because of behavior caused by a person's disability.) [ALP S-057]

(10)The Passenger is smoking and has refused the Driver's request to stop. If the Driver has already begun the trip the Driver can discharge the Passenger in a safe location. (CAUTION: The Driver must ask the Passenger at least twice to stop smoking before requiring him or her to leave the Vehicle.) [ALP S-011]
(11)The Vehicle is a Street Hail Livery and the Passenger is in the Hail Exclusionary Zone. [ALP S-011]
(12)The Vehicle is a For-Hire Vehicle and the Passengers is attempting to Hail the Driver. [ALP S-011]
(13)The Vehicle is a Street Hail Livery and the Driver has accepted a dispatch call for a Pre- Arranged Trip and is logged on but unavailable for street-hail in the Technology System. [ALP S-083]
(14)The Vehicle is a For-Hire Vehicle and the Driver has accepted a dispatch call for a Pre- Arranged Trip. [ALP S-011]

(15)The Vehicle is a Taxicab or Street Hail Livery and the Driver has accepted a trip from the accessible dispatch program pursuant to 35 RCNY
§
53-08 or an Accessible E-Hail Request from an approved E-Hail App and is logged on but unavailable for street-hail in the Technology System. [ALP S-109]

(Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016; amended City Record 12/15/2016, eff. 1/14/2017; amended City Record 5/17/2018, eff. 6/16/2018; amended City Record 12/9/2020, eff. 1/8/2021; amended City Record 2/2/2023, eff. 3/4/2023; amended City Record 8/22/2024, eff. 9/21/2024; amended City Record 4/2/2025, eff. 5/2/2025) [ALP S-109]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-21 Operations – Drivers from Issuing Jurisdictions.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-21 Operations – Drivers from Issuing Jurisdictions.

PARA.CHARFORMAT: A driver holding a Valid Issuing Jurisdiction driver's license (a IDL) will be eligible for Reciprocity, provided that the driver meets all of the following requirements:

RECORD.PARA: (1)The driver is operating a vehicle that is licensed to operate for-hire by an Issuing Jurisdiction (see 35 RCNY § 59A-05). [ALP S-011]
(2)The driver holds a Valid Chauffeur's License. [ALP S-011]
(3)While operating within New York City, the Valid Chauffeur's License must be displayed on request or be posted within the vehicle. [ALP S-011]
(4)The driver maintains and completes the Trip Record required and described in subdivision (e), below. [ALP S-011]

(5)A driver providing For-Hire transportation in New York City under Reciprocal Recognition must comply with the Rules of this chapter while operating within New York City as if the driver were licensed by the Commission. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: A driver who meets the requirements above for Reciprocity will be allowed, without any License issued by or any fee paid to the Commission, to do the following:

RECORD.PARA: (1)Pick up Passengers in the driver's Issuing Jurisdiction for travel to or through New York City. [ALP S-011]
(2)Pick up Passengers in New York City for travel to the driver's Issuing Jurisdiction. [ALP S-011]

(3)When transporting Passengers between the jurisdictions (as described in paragraphs (1) or (2) above), temporarily discharge and pick up such Passengers within New York City; provided, however, that all the stops must occur within 24 hours of the initial pick up of the Passengers. [ALP S-011]
(4)Transit through New York City for a trip beginning and ending outside New York City. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: A driver must NOT provide transportation for hire between two points wholly within New York City unless both vehicle and driver are licensed by the Commission.
A driver is engaged in Unlicensed Activity if he or she:

RECORD.PARA: (1)Fails to have the licenses required or is not eligible for Reciprocity under subdivision (a) of this section. [ALP S-011]
(2)Engages in the prohibited activity described in subdivision (c) of this section. [ALP S-011]
(1)A record of each trip between New York City and an Issuing Jurisdiction must be made prior to the commencement of the trip and recorded on a Trip Record carried in the vehicle. [ALP S-011]
(2)The Trip Record must be kept for a period of no less than one year after the trip. [ALP S-011]
(3)The record of each trip must include the following information: [ALP S-011]
(i)The Passenger's name or other identifier. [ALP S-011]
(ii)The time of scheduled pick-up. [ALP S-011]
(iii)The location where the Passenger is scheduled to be picked up. [ALP S-011]
(iv)The location of any intermediate stops between the original pick-up location and the final destination. [ALP S-011]
(v)The Passenger's final destination. [ALP S-011]
(vi)At the completion of the trip, the time of completion shall be added to the record. [ALP S-011]
(4)The Trip Record must be kept in the vehicle during any trip between New York City and an Issuing Jurisdiction, including a trip through either New York City or an Issuing Jurisdiction. [ALP S-011]

(5)The Trip Record must be presented for inspection on request to any police officer or peace officer acting pursuant to his or her special duties or other person authorized by the Commission or by the Issuing Jurisdiction. [ALP S-011]
(6)Failure to present a properly maintained Trip Record when requested by an authorized person will be presumptive evidence of Unlicensed Activity. [ALP S-011]
(Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016) [ALP S-011]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-22 Vehicles – Operation and Condition.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-22 Vehicles – Operation and Condition.

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Drivers must comply with the New York City Air Pollution Control Code, including the provision that the Driver must not cause or permit the engine of the Vehicle to idle for longer than three minutes.
Inspection by Driver of Vehicle Condition.

A Driver must not operate a Vehicle without continuing personal inspection and reasonable determination that all equipment, including brakes, tires, lights, an Approved Tablet, signals and passenger seatbelts and shoulder belts, is in good working order.
Fine: $50 if plead guilty before a hearing; $75 if found guilty following a hearing.
During his or her work shift, a Driver must keep the Vehicle's interior clean.
Required Exterior Identification Clean and Visible.
During his or her work shift, a Driver must keep all exterior markings required by the Commission clean and unobstructed so that they can be seen at all times.
No Unauthorized Equipment in Taxicabs and Street Hail Liveries.

A Driver must not operate a Taxicab or Street Hail Livery that uses or has installed any equipment or mechanical or electronic devices not specifically listed in these rules, unless authorized in writing by the Commission.
Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.
Use of Electronic Devices in Taxicabs.

A Driver may use no more than one electronic device with an E-Hail Application, separate from the Driver Information Monitor supplied as part of the Technology System and/or a device provided for the Accessible Dispatch Program.
Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.
Use of Electronic Devices in Street Hail Liveries.
In addition to the Technology System and/or a device provided for the Accessible Dispatch Program, a Street Hail Livery is permitted to have:

RECORD.PARA: (1)the dispatch equipment required by its Street Hail Livery Base; and [ALP S-011]

(2)one electronic device that is used to accept E-Hails from a licensed E-Hail Application and/or dispatches from a Base, provided that the device is mounted in a fixed position and not hand-held and use of the electronic device is limited to either voice or one-touch preprogrammed buttons or keys while the Vehicle is in motion. [ALP S-057]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.
Use of Electronic Devices in For-Hire Vehicles.

In addition to the dispatch equipment required by a Driver's Base, a For-Hire Vehicle Driver is permitted to use the following electronic device(s) provided that the device(s) is mounted in a fixed position and not hand-held and use of the electronic device(s) is limited to either voice or one-touch preprogrammed buttons or keys while the Vehicle is in motion:

RECORD.PARA: (1)If the dispatch equipment required by a Driver's Base is capable of accepting dispatches from other Bases, ONE electronic device that is used to accept dispatches from a Baser. [ALP S-057]
(2)If the dispatch equipment required by a Driver's Base is not capable of accepting dispatches from other Bases, TWO electronic devices that are used to accept dispatches from a Base. [ALP S-057]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.
A Driver must not place any signs in a Taxicab or a Street Hail Livery not specifically listed in these rules, unless authorized in writing by the Commission.

RECORD.PARA: (j) IVCS. A Driver must not delete, modify, or alter in any way image, or video footage of the IVCS. [ALP S-090]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $250 and/or minimum 5 day to maximum 30 day suspension.

RECORD.PARA: (k) Use of Approved Tablets in For-Hire Vehicles. A For-Hire Vehicle Driver is permitted one Approved Tablet per For-Hire Vehicle, pursuant to 35 RCNY § 59E-22(i). The tablet must be mounted in a fixed position and not hand-held.

COL.PARA: Fine: First violation: $250 if plead guilty before a hearing; $350 if found guilty following a hearing. Second and subsequent violations: $350 if plead guilty before a hearing; $450 if found guilty following a hearing. [ALP S-107]

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016; amended City Record 5/17/2018, eff. 6/16/2018; amended City Record 12/9/2020, eff. 1/8/2021; amended City Record 9/22/2023, eff. 10/22/2023; amended City Record 2/4/2025, eff. 3/6/2025) [ALP S-107]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-23 Vehicles – Required Items in Taxicabs and Street Hail Liveries.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-23 Vehicles – Required Items in Taxicabs and Street Hail Liveries.
(a)A Driver must not operate a Taxicab or Street Hail Livery unless the Vehicle is equipped or provided with the following: [ALP S-011]
(1)The Technology System (or a written Trip Record, when required and permitted). [ALP S-026]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $150 if plead guilty before a hearing; $200 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (2)The Driver's TLC Driver License (or Paratransit License if the Vehicle is an Accessible Street Hail Livery being operated by a Paratransit Driver) displayed in the appropriate license frame). [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (3)The Rate Card assigned to the Taxicab or Street Hail Livery displayed in the Rate Card frame. [ALP S-011]
(4)A New York City five (5) borough indexed street map or geographic position system device. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $25 if plead guilty before a hearing; $50 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (1)A Driver must not block a Passenger's view of the Taximeter, his or her TLC Driver License, or the Rate Card and must not block in any way a Passenger's access to the medallion number or Street Hail Livery License number on the Raised Lettering Plaque and the Identification Braille Plaque. [ALP S-026]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (2)A Driver must not operate a Taxicab or Street Hail Livery after sunset unless the face of the Taximeter, his or her TLC Driver License, and the Rate Card are illuminated so that they are clearly visible from the rear seat by a Passenger with normal vision. [ALP S-011]
(Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016; amended City Record 5/17/2018, eff. 6/16/2018) [ALP S-026]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-24 Vehicles – Required Items in For-Hire Vehicles.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-24 Vehicles – Required Items in For-Hire Vehicles.

PARA.CHARFORMAT: A Driver must not operate a For-Hire Vehicle without the following:

RECORD.PARA: (1)On the right visor, on top of the right side of the dashboard or in the glove compartment: [ALP S-011]
(i)The certificate of registration or a legible copy of it; and [ALP S-011]
(ii)The insurance card or a legible copy of it. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $150 if plead guilty before a hearing; $200 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (2)The Driver's TLC Driver License displayed in a protective holder attached to the back of the driver's seat in the Vehicle. [ALP S-011]

(i)Exception for Black Cars and Luxury Limousines. Black Cars and Luxury Limousines will not be required to have the protective holder mounted to the back of the driver's seat as required in this paragraph. However, the Driver's TLC Driver License must be displayed in the Vehicle in a way so as to be clearly visible from the Passenger seat and available for inspection by the Passenger upon request. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $150 if plead guilty before a hearing; $200 if found guilty following a hearing.

If the For-Hire Vehicle is used for providing pre-arranged transportation for hire between the City of New York and an Issuing Jurisdiction, a Trip Record, as described in 35 RCNY § 80-25(f)(3), must be carried in the Vehicle.
A Driver must not block a Passenger's view of his or her TLC Driver License and, in a Livery, the Livery Passengers' bill of rights.
Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016) [ALP S-011]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-25 Vehicles – Technology System Operation.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-25 Vehicles – Technology System Operation.

(a)A Driver is required to log in and operate the Technology System for each trip in a Taxicab or Street Hail Livery, including those Taxicab or Street Hail Livery trips that begin with the use of an E-Hail Application but otherwise excluding Street Hail Livery trips that are Pre-Arranged Trips, except for Flex Fare Trips. [ALP S-101]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $250 and/or minimum 5 day to maximum 30 day suspension.
Log Off Procedures for a Taxicab and Street Hail Livery.

RECORD.PARA: (1)A Driver must log off from the Technology System. [ALP S-083]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.
Unavailable Procedures for a Taxicab and Street Hail Livery.
Upon accepting an Accessible E-Hail Request or a trip from the Dispatch Program pursuant to 35 RCNY
53-08 or a Licensed E-Hail Application or a Pre-Arranged Trip, a Driver must be logged on but unavailable for street hail in the Technology System.
Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (d)If necessary, the Driver will transmit any corrections electronically, using the Technology System. [ALP S-083]

(e) Technology System(s) Malfunction. In the event the Technology System malfunctions or fails to operate, the Driver must fix the Technology System before accepting any new trips to transport passengers. [ALP S-083]
(1)When correcting a written Trip Record, a Driver: [ALP S-083]
(i)Must not erase any entry or make it completely unreadable. [ALP S-011]
(ii)Must correct a wrong entry only by drawing a single line through the incorrect entry and recording the date, time and reason for the change. [ALP S-011]
(iii)Must not leave blank lines between entries on any written Trip Record. [ALP S-011]
(2)A Driver must not rewrite a written Trip Record either in whole or in part, unless authorized by the Commission. [ALP S-083]
(1)A Driver must not connect any unauthorized device to the Technology System. [ALP S-026]
(2)The Driver must not tamper with, repair or attempt to repair the Technology System seals, cable connections or electrical wiring, [ALP S-026]
(3)The Driver must not make any change in the Taxicab's or Street Hail Livery's mechanism or its tires which would affect the operation of the Technology System. [ALP S-026]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016; amended City Record 5/17/2018, eff. 6/16/2018; amended City Record 2/2/2023, eff. 3/4/2023; amended City Record 8/22/2024, eff. 9/21/2024; amended City Record 4/2/2025, eff. 5/2/2025) [ALP S-109]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-26 Vehicle – Taxicab and Street Hail Livery Taximeters and Roof Lights.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-26 Vehicle – Taxicab and Street Hail Livery Taximeters and Roof Lights.

PARA.CHARFORMAT: A Driver must not pick up or transport a Passenger in a Taxi or Street Hail Livery when the Taximeter in his or her Taxicab or Street Hail Livery is defective.
Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.
If the Taximeter breaks down during a trip so that the fare can no longer be monitored, the Driver must immediately tell the Passenger and offer him or her the option of:

RECORD.PARA: (i)Continuing the trip after mutually agreeing to a reasonable fare, or [ALP S-011]
(ii)Terminating the trip and paying the fare shown on the Taximeter to that point. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.
Procedures for Terminating Use of Vehicle with Defective Taximeter.
Upon terminating a trip because of a defective Taximeter the Driver must:

RECORD.PARA: (i)Log off from the Technology System [ALP S-083]
(ii)Lock the rear doors [ALP S-011]
(iii)Transmit data that the Taximeter is defective (or enter on a written Trip Record, if the Technology System is inoperative) [ALP S-026]
(iv)Return the Vehicle immediately to the garage or base of record or a licensed Taximeter repair shop. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $75 if plead guilty before a hearing; $100 if found guilty following a hearing.

RECORD.PARA: (4)A Driver must immediately notify the Taxicab Owner of any Taximeter defect. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: A Driver must not drive a Taxicab or Street Hail Livery unless all Taximeter seals and cable housing seals are in good condition, pressed by the Commission or its authorized designee and show no signs of tampering. The serial number of the Taximeter must be the same as that shown on the rate card assigned to the Vehicle.

RECORD.PARA: (i)The Commission will assume that a Driver who operates a Vehicle with a broken Taximeter seal knew of the tampering or alteration and deliberately operated the Vehicle in violation of this Rule, and will take appropriate action against the Driver. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: A Driver must not pick up or transport a Passenger unless the Taximeter is properly equipped with paper for printing receipts.

As soon as a Passenger enters the Taxicab or the Street Hail Livery on a Hail Trip, the Driver must immediately place the Taximeter in the recording or "Hired" position and must keep it in that position until arrival at the destination.

If the Passenger is a Person with a Disability, the Driver must not place the Taximeter in the recording or "Hired" position while the Driver is assisting a person with a disability to enter the Vehicle or while assisting with that Passenger's mobility aid.

If the Passenger is an individual with a disability who requires assistance to exit the Vehicle, the Driver must place the Taximeter in a non-recording position before assisting such Passenger and must leave the Taximeter in a non-recording position until such Passenger has paid the fare and safely exited the Vehicle.

When the Taxicab or Street Hail Livery is engaged in a flat fare trip between Kennedy Airport and Manhattan, the Driver must key the information into the Technology System, as applicable (or enter on a written Trip Record) that this is a flat fare trip to or from Kennedy Airport and include the time and distance of the trip.

Upon reaching the Passenger's destination, the Driver must place the Taximeter in a non-recording or "Time Off" position, inform the Passenger of the fare due and leave the Taximeter in a non- recording position until the fare is paid.
Immediately after the Passenger leaves the Vehicle, the Driver must clear the Taximeter, placing it in an off or "Vacant" position in which it must remain until the next Passenger enters the Vehicle.

RECORD.PARA: (1)A Driver must not connect any unauthorized device to the Taximeter. [ALP S-011]
(2)The Driver must not tamper with, repair or attempt to repair the Taximeter seals, cable connections or electrical wiring, [ALP S-011]
(3)The Driver must not make any change in the Vehicle's mechanism or its tires which would affect the operation of the Taximeter system. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $50 - $350 and/or suspension up to 30 days.

RECORD.PARA: (4)It will be an affirmative defense to a violation of this provision, 35 RCNY § 80-26(c), that the Driver: [ALP S-011]
(i)Did not know of or participate in the alleged Taximeter tampering, and [ALP S-011]
(ii)Exercised due diligence to ensure that Taximeter tampering did not occur. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Report Signs of Tampering to TLC.

A Driver must notify the Owner (or, in the case of a Street Hail Livery, the Street Hail Livery Base) and the Commission by telephone immediately, and in writing within 24 hours, upon the discovery of any of the following:

RECORD.PARA: (1)A Taximeter other than the Taximeter approved by the Commission and indicated on the Rate Card, has been installed in the Vehicle operated by the Driver. [ALP S-011]
(2)A Taximeter seal in the Vehicle operated by the Driver has been removed or tampered with. [ALP S-011]

(3)An unauthorized device that may affect the operation of a Taximeter has been connected to the Taximeter, seal, cable connection or electrical wiring of the Vehicle operated by the Driver. [ALP S-011]

(4)Any intervening connections, splices, "Y" connections or direct or indirect interruptions or connections of any kind whatsoever have been discovered on any wiring harness attached to the Taximeter in the Vehicle operated by the Driver. [ALP S-011]
(1)The Roof Light must be illuminated when the Taximeter is not in use and the Driver is on duty and available; and [ALP S-011]
(2)The Roof Light must be off when any of the following occurs: [ALP S-011]
(i)The Taximeter is in use. [ALP S-011]
(ii)The Driver has logged off or logged on but is unavailable for street-hail in the Technology System. [ALP S-083]
(iii)The Driver is a Street Hail Livery Driver traveling to pick up a Passenger for a Pre-Arranged Trip. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $100 if plead guilty before a hearing; $150 if found guilty following a hearing.
Unauthorized Roof Light Devices or Repairs.

RECORD.PARA: (1)A Driver must not tamper with the Roof Light or any of the interior lights or connections except to replace a defective bulb or fuse. [ALP S-011]
(2)The Roof Light of a Taxicab or Street Hail Livery must be automatically controlled only by the movement of the Taximeter button or ignition switch. [ALP S-011]

(3)The Commission will assume that a Driver who operates a Vehicle with an unauthorized installation or device controlling interior or roof lighting knew of the unauthorized installation or device and deliberately operated the Vehicle in violation of this Rule, and the Commission will take appropriate action against the Driver. [ALP S-011]

PARA.CHARFORMAT: Fine: $50 - $350 and/or suspension up to 30 days.

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016; amended City Record 5/17/2018, eff. 6/16/2018; amended City Record 2/2/2023, eff. 3/4/2023) [ALP S-083]

RECORD.HEADING: § 80-27 Persistent Violator Program.
RECORD.PARA: § 80-27 Persistent Violator Program.
(a) Program for Persistent Violators of Drivers' Rules. [ALP S-086]
(1) Suspension and Revocation – Points and Time Periods. [ALP S-086]

(i) Suspension. Any Driver whose License has not been revoked and who has within a 15-month period accumulated a total of at least six but fewer than ten TLC Points or DMV Points or both combined, counting from the date of conviction, will have such Driver's TLC Driver License suspended for up to 30 days. [ALP S-086]

(ii) Revocation. The Commission will revoke the TLC Driver License of any Driver who has within a 15-month period accumulated a total of 10 or more TLC Points or DMV Points or both combined, counting from the date of conviction. [ALP S-086]

(2) Multiple Violations from a Single Incident. For a Driver who has been found guilty of multiple violations arising from a single incident, the Commission will consider the Driver guilty of the single violation with the highest point total for purposes of this section. [ALP S-086]
(3) More Severe Penalties at Discretion of Commission. The Commission can impose penalties that are more severe than or additional to those stated in this section. [ALP S-086]
(4) Hearing Process and Imposition of Penalties. [ALP S-086]
(i)The Commission will impose penalties described in this section following the hearing at which the Driver is found to have violated the rule(s) triggering these penalties. [ALP S-086]
(ii)Persistent violator penalties will be in addition to those penalties specifically listed in the Rules. [ALP S-086]
(5) Point Reduction for Voluntary Course Completion. [ALP S-086]

(i)Before suspending or revoking a Driver's License, the Commission will deduct three TLC Points from the points accumulated by any Licensee who voluntarily attends and satisfactorily completes a Point Reduction Course or three DMV Points from the points accumulated by any Licensee who voluntarily attends and satisfactorily completes a Defensive Driving Course. Such point reduction will count only towards points accumulated by the Licensee as a result of the conviction for violations that occurred within 15 months prior to the date of the completion of the course, counting from the date of conviction. [ALP S-086]

(ii)In order for the Defensive Driving Course or Point Reduction Course to reduce the Driver's Persistent Violator Program points, the course must be satisfactorily completed prior to the adjudication of the Persistent Violator summons. Completion of the Defensive Driving Course or Point Reduction Course after the adjudication of a Persistent Violator Program summons will reduce the Driver's Persistent Violator Program points only after the Driver completes such Driver's suspension. If the Driver's License was issued by a state other than New York, the Driver must submit a recent driving abstract from the state of licensure. The Driver must furnish the Commission with proof of when the course was taken and that the course was satisfactorily completed. [ALP S-086]
(iii)If the Commission has already suspended or revoked the Driver's License, the point reduction will not change the Commission's decision. [ALP S-086]

(iv)The Commission will not reduce the TLC Points of any Driver more than once in any five year period and will not reduce the DMV Points of any Driver more than once in any 18-month period. [ALP S-086]

(v)If no Point Reduction Course is available when the Driver seeks to enroll, the Driver may take a Defensive Driving Course. After the Driver furnishes the Commission with proof that the course was satisfactorily completed, the TLC will deduct three points from the number of points accrued under the Persistent Violator Program whether such points are TLC Points or DMV Points. [ALP S-086]
(Added City Record 9/22/2016, eff. 10/22/2016; amended City Record 12/9/2020, eff. 1/8/2021; amended City Record 5/10/2023, eff. 6/9/2023) [ALP S-086]
